mentions.raw_name: Floppy woolen hat patched with leather
Gilded figurehead (maiden with a bowl of fruit)
Arya Stark (also: Salty, Squab, Nan, Weasel, Arry)
Ternesio Terys (Tradesman-Captain of the Titan's Daughter)
The waif (acolyte at House of Black and White)
The kindly man (priest at House of Black and White)
Young man by the pool (dying, in silvery cloak)
House Frey (the Freys murdered her mother)
Faceless Men (the House of Black and White is their temple)
Brotherhood Without Banners (Lem, Anguy, Tom o' Sevens — implied by reference to the hollow hill)
The Many-Faced God (worshipped at the House of Black and White; "faces beyond count")
Faith of the Seven (Arya: "they are not my Seven"; Sept-Beyond-the-Sea in Braavos; "the southron god, the one with seven faces")
The Moonsingers (led the Braavosi to refuge; greatest temple in Braavos)
R'hllor / Lord of Light (red priests; Red Temple in Braavos; Thoros of Myr)
Old Gods of the North (Arya thinks "the old gods are dead"; associated with weirwoods and godswoods)
Father of Waters (his house is built anew whenever he takes his bride)
Ibbenese (big-bellied whalers out of Ibben in the harbor)
Summer Islanders (swan ships from the Summer Isles)
Floppy woolen hat patched with leather (crew gift)
Stone cups (at the black pool rim)
Gilded figurehead — maiden with bowl of fruit (Titan's Daughter prow)
Iron brazier (atop Red Temple, twenty feet across)
Face-changing (the kindly man's skull face dissolves into a kindly old man; Jaqen H'ghar described with distinctive white-and-red hair, connected to face-changing)
"Valar morghulis" / "Valar dohaeris" (ritual phrases — "all men must die" / "all men must serve")
The black pool (people drink from it and then die or go to alcoves to die — appears to offer a peaceful death)
Candle scent that shifts (smells of snow, pine needles, and hot stew — an unfamiliar, possibly magical incense)
Doors that open without human hand (the weirwood-and-ebony doors of the House of Black and White)
The Titan's roar (mechanical — "a terrible groaning and grinding" to alert the Arsenal)
Thoros of Myr's kiss bringing Lord Beric back from death (referenced)
Sealord (the Sealords of Braavos — statues along the canal; customs officers at Chequy Port)
Titan's Daughter (galleas — the ship Arya crossed on; purple sails, double bank of oars, three tall masts)
